column45: I have been self employed most of my professional life and ran a high stress lifestyle. I have had 2 divorces which was also impactful on my mental health.   I started to search for answers a few years ago as I noticed my ability to make decisions had deteriorated, possibly from stress. I began to develop tremors in my right arm mid this year and am now being treated for PD. Due to medication my tremors are lessoning but still present.   I am having some side effects from my medications, mostly constipation and ED which is now subsided but I am taking Testosterone.   I can record my mental alertness levels and change of ability to accomplish tasks if it is helpful. I do notice I am very slow to get things done and my ability to carry on an in-depth conversation is greatly reduced. I fluctuate in my ability to have a lively conversation.   I sense that my will power to resist certain things has been reduced. I normally can easily avoid alcohol when I feel it is not a good time to drink but lately if others are around I have not been able to resist the temptation.  I do not have a drinking issue but I wanted to avoid drinks as I am lined up for surgery on my knee on Oct 27th.  Other things to know about me;  - I have hearing aids and hearing issues at times.  - I am having my left knee replaced Oct 27th through a private clinic.   - I have ADHD which I have been able to work with over my life.  - If you want more info on my journey with PD, please ask.

My Mother at 72 years and my oldest brother at 49 died of pancreatic cancer which doctors thought was the result of herbicides and pesticides on the farm in southern Alberta.  Having been on HRT for 9 years, I believe this has caused liver damage NAFLD because the hormones were not balanced (1992 - 2001) due to ignorance of doctors.   I feel the Chem Trails in our skies also attribute to brain diseases due to the use of presence of aluminum and barium etc.  Mercury in the body due to dental fillings and its presence in the air due to coal burning that is used for making electricity in Alberta.

Rosuvastatin 20 mg 1 comprimé par jour, amlodipine 10 mg 1 par jour,levocarb 100+25 mg 1.5 comprim 3 fois par jour finalementlevocarb cr 200-50 mg 1 comprimé par jour. J'espère vraiment fair parti d'une étude pour voir si ma situation peut-être amilioré Merci et au plaisir de vous voir.

column45: Motivée pour participer aux projets de recherche à Québec et à Montréal. A des périodes off très importants et utilise deux cannes pour marcher. Ne connaît pas si les membres de son famille du côté de son père fait le maladie de Parkinson, mais personne de son côté maternel fait le maladie de Parkinson. Fait des migraines importantes, et a fait des pertes de visions à cause des migraines. Était avec Dre Mélanie Langlois, mais elle est en train de changer de neurologue pour Dr Ronald Postuma.

column45: Did recreational drugs at university then stopped. Tried Sante Cannabis in an attempt to improve sleep and to address pain.    Took:	CBD 25:1  		CBD 10:13  		Nabilone 0.5 mg  Didn't notice any improvement, although sleep was better with the nabilone.  Gets back aches from bad posture.  Short term memory is not as bad as my long term (part of the issue might be lack of attention)  Lost sense of smell 5 to 7 years before diagnosis.  Played hockey as a kid (1 concussion), and again as an adult for about 15 years (checked into the boards once lost consciousness for a few seconds) did a lot of skiing when younger. At University played varsity ice hockey, basketball, volleyball and tennis. Played competitive squash and taught tennis and squash.

column45: I fall asleep readily, but wake regularly during the night to urinate. column45: My older sister died at the age of 44years of an undiagnoised neurodegenerative disease.  We know she suffered from bladder, bowel issues, progressive muscle weakness and breathing issues. Her heart  and kidneys gave out. She was in ICU when her heart stopped and they couldn't restart her it.   My Grandma's muscles weakened, couldn't hold her head upright, it would fall back  with her mouth open and she eventually couldn't swallow, that's how she died. No food or water.  As with my sister we have similar challenges.
